piece.title: Rebecca
piece.notes: A 10-minute romantic piece for Rebecca. Strings-forward, no piano. G major — warm, open, and resonant under guitar or voice. Emotional arc: quiet arrival → growing tenderness → full warmth → bloom at center → a breath of stillness → return with memory → gentle evening close. Manuel plays guitar or sings over the bed. The generated track is the embrace; his instrument is the voice in it.

sections.name: approach
sections.notes: She doesn't know it's about to begin. Sparse strings open slowly — single notes, long tones. The I-V-vi-IV progression is deliberately familiar and warm. Pedal bass barely moves. Leave room for silence.

sections.name: tenderness
sections.notes: The piece finds its voice. Lyrical melody enters — this is where Manuel's guitar or voice belongs. Counterpoint strings enter underneath, very soft. Root-fifth bass gives gentle movement. The progression is the most natural, loving thing in music — nothing surprising, everything true.

sections.name: warmth
sections.notes: Longest lyrical section — 24 bars of sustained warmth. The ii chord adds a gentle ache (borrowed longing). Walking bass gives the section forward motion without urgency. Breath arc means it swells and recedes naturally — like a long exhale. This is the section to play guitar over.

sections.name: bloom
sections.notes: The emotional peak. The iii chord (B minor in G) appears for the first time — a touch of shadow that makes the resolution back to I feel earned. Full density, developed melody, walking bass. This is the center of the song — where it says what it has been building toward. No counterpoint here; the developed motif carries it.

sections.name: stillness
sections.notes: A quiet breath after the bloom. Sparse texture, pedal bass, generative melody wanders gently — like watching light move. I-IV-I is a rocking motion, almost lullaby-like. This is where a listener catches their breath before the return.

sections.name: return
sections.notes: The melody returns — now it feels like memory. Same lyrical behavior but the motif has been through augmentation; it moves slower, fuller. Counterpoint is dissonance-free here — pure consonance, nothing unresolved. The added IV at the end begins the farewell.

sections.name: evening
sections.notes: The piece does not end — it simply becomes quiet. Sparse melody, root-only bass, long slow fade. The I-V-I-IV-I progression is the most resolved thing in tonal music. By the end only the strings remain, then silence. Give this section long reverb tails in Logic.
